name: Sigstore Transparency API (Rekor)
what_it_does: Open-source software supply chain security framework with REST API (Rekor transparency log) for artifact signing, verification, and immutable audit logging of signing events. Used by default in npm, PyPI, and Kubernetes ecosystem.
use_cases: Verifying software artifact signatures before deployment in CI/CD pipelines
Logging artifact signing events to immutable Rekor transparency log
Querying signing history for artifacts to audit supply chain provenance
Integrating keyless signing (via OIDC) into build automation agents
Policy enforcement: blocking deployment of unsigned or unverified artifacts
not_for: Enterprise PKI requirements needing commercial CA (use DigiCert instead)
Long-term private key management (Sigstore uses ephemeral keys by design)
Non-technical policy enforcement without supporting tooling
best_when: An agent enforces software supply chain integrity by verifying artifact signatures and provenance before deployment, especially in Kubernetes environments.
avoid_when: You need traditional long-lived signing certificates — Sigstore uses ephemeral keys tied to OIDC identity.

auth.notes: Rekor transparency log API is public and unauthenticated for reads. Writes (log entries) use OIDC tokens from identity providers (GitHub Actions, Google, Microsoft). Fulcio CA uses OIDC for certificate issuance.

pricing.paid_tiers: Self-hosted: run your own Rekor instance; no commercial offering
pricing.notes: Sigstore is Linux Foundation project — free public infrastructure. Production use of public instance is acceptable. Self-hosting available for air-gapped or compliance-sensitive environments.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits on public Rekor; reasonable usage expected

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published for Rekor API. REST API is straightforward for log queries. Error responses are JSON. Cosign CLI is the primary interface; direct API use is lower-level.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transparency log entries are append-only and immutable. Verification queries are inherently idempotent read operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Sigstore uses ephemeral keys — certificates expire after signing; no long-term key management
OIDC token required for signing — agents need identity provider integration
Transparency log entries are public and permanent — all signatures are publicly auditable
cosign CLI wraps Rekor/Fulcio APIs — most agents should use cosign rather than raw API

security_score_components.security_notes: Linux Foundation security governance. Keyless signing using OIDC prevents key compromise. Public transparency log enables audit and detection of unauthorized signing. CNCF incubating project. Widely adopted by major package registries.
